title: clematis iranica
intro: Clematis iranica, also known as the Iranian clematis or Pashtun clematis, is a perennial flowering plant that belongs to the Ranunculaceae family, characterized by its showy purple flowers and long, twining stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to its alkaloid and glycoside content. The health benefits of Clematis iranica include its potential to reduce stress and anxiety, improve sleep quality, and alleviate symptoms of fever and rheumatism. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally or applied topically to the skin.
